- Uses querySelector with OUIA selectors to interact with components
- Asserts on element roles, aria attributes, or data attributes that changed
If any test would break due to your changes, fix it. Common test fixes:
- Wrap assertions in waitFor() when content renders via portal
- Add popperProps={{ appendTo: 'inline' }} to component renders in tests
- Update getByRole/getByText queries to match new attribute values

Apply ALL of the following 5 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 167
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-dropdown' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-dropdown__group

Code context:
```
  166  /*remove bullets from dropdown*/
  167  .pf-v5-c-dropdown__group>ul {
  168      list-style: none;

```

### Fix 2
Line: 167
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown-group]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown__group' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-dropdown__group' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-dropdown__group

Code context:
```
  166  /*remove bullets from dropdown*/
  167  .pf-v5-c-dropdown__group>ul {
  168      list-style: none;

```

### Fix 3
Line: 167
Rule [sd-css-removed-dropdown]:
This CSS references the 'pf-c-dropdown' component class which was removed in PatternFly v6.

The Dropdown component was rebuilt and no longer uses this CSS class. This CSS override is dead and should be removed.

Check if the behavior you were overriding is now available via a component prop instead.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-dropdown__group

Code context:
```
  166  /*remove bullets from dropdown*/
  167  .pf-v5-c-dropdown__group>ul {
  168      list-style: none;

```

Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-dropdown' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-dropdown__group

Code context:
```
  166  /*remove bullets from dropdown*/
  167  .pf-v5-c-dropdown__group>ul {
  168      list-style: none;

```

### Fix 5

The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-dropdown__group' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-dropdown__group' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-dropdown__group

Code context:
```
  166  /*remove bullets from dropdown*/
  167  .pf-v5-c-dropdown__group>ul {
  168      list-style: none;

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/components/netflow-traffic.css
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the CSS file to understand the context around line 167.
Now let me read the test file:

Now I have all the context I need. Let me reason through the fixes:

**All 5 fixes target the same code block** — lines 166–170:
```css
/*remove bullets from dropdown*/
.pf-v5-c-dropdown__group>ul {
    list-style: none;
    padding-left: 10px;
}
```

- Fix 1: `pf-v5-c-dropdown` class was removed, no v6 equivalent
- Fix 2: `pf-v5-c-dropdown__group` class was removed, no v6 equivalent  
- Fix 3: The Dropdown component was rebuilt; this CSS override is dead
- Fix 4: Same as Fix 1 — `pf-v5-c-dropdown` removed entirely
- Fix 5: Same as Fix 2 — `pf-v5-c-dropdown__group` removed entirely

All 5 fixes unanimously say: the `pf-v5-c-dropdown__group` class no longer exists in PatternFly v6. The Dropdown was completely rebuilt. This CSS rule block is dead code and should be removed entirely.

**Test file analysis:** The test file doesn't reference `pf-v5-c-dropdown__group`, doesn't query for dropdown group elements by class, and doesn't assert on any styling related to this CSS rule. No test changes needed.

Let me apply the fix:

The dead CSS block has been cleanly removed. The file flows correctly from the `.hidden` rule to the `#netflow-warning` rule.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Removed the entire dead CSS rule block (lines 166–170: comment + `.pf-v5-c-dropdown__group>ul` selector and its declarations). The `pf-v5-c-dropdown` class no longer exists in PatternFly v6. |
| Fix 2 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-dropdown-group` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — the `pf-v5-c-dropdown__group` class was part of the same deleted rule block. |
| Fix 3 | `sd-css-removed-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — the Dropdown component was rebuilt in v6; this CSS override is dead. |
| Fix 4 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — no `pf-v6-c-dropdown` equivalent exists. |
| Fix 5 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-dropdown-group` | ✅ Applied | Same removal — no `pf-v6-c-dropdown__group` equivalent exists. |

**Test file:** `/home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/components/__tests__/netflow-traffic.spec.tsx` — ✅ No changes needed. The test does not reference `pf-v5-c-dropdown__group` or any dropdown group styling. All assertions target element IDs (`#refresh-dropdown-container`, `#refresh-button`, `#filter-toolbar`) and API call counts, none of which are affected by this CSS removal.
